title: Were the Seeds ever designed to be animate
display_title: Were the Seeds ever designed to be animate
wikitext:
[]: Were the Seeds ever designed to be animate?             Quote Post by Replicator » Wed Jan 02, 2013 9:55 am This is a question I've always had since watching NGE. Were Adam and Lilith ever supposed to be animate, or interact with their progeny? The Seeds technically have souls, and are godlike in power. Aside from what SEELE was doing, using as a mediums to trigger 3I and all, could the Seeds have wills of their own and choose to 'live amongst' their offspring? Like, were the Seeds meant to be in the open knowledge of the children they produced? Lilith is crucified in the Black Moon, but we don't know if this was its original state (from arriving on Earth) or was placed by there as a restraint? Adam "walks around" during 2I, but we don't know how much of this was on its own accord, than the circumstances of the time. Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by Electreel » Wed Jan 02, 2013 11:18 am Interesting question. Seeds were clearly made to be animated for an indefinite amount of time (as you pointed out they have souls, and their bodies are perfectly able to accomplish that), I´m not sure if they would have free will, or if they would be able to contact their progeny though. We know practically nothing about their initial states - Adam was put into suspended animation as soon as Lilith landed on Earth, and about Lilith... well, there´s this theory of Reichu that she may have looked Adam-like initially, and then she turned into a plain "chaneller" of LCL and souls from the Dirac sea. Maybe that´s what every seed is eventually bound to become: an inanimate "bringer of life".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 02, 2013 12:48 pm I'm not sure why the First Ancestral Race would have created progenitor beings that are basically giant people, containing the souls of their own, if the Seeds weren't at least intended to have the potential to be more than life-generating automatons. A bit like in Nausicaa (the manga)... The title character is pondering the origins of the bioengineered God Warriors, giant humanoids of immense power that razed the world to the ground in times past. She had thought that they were created to be mere weapons, but, after dealing with a God Warrior individual she names Ohma, she discovers that they're actually intelligent, in a way unbefitting a mere weapon. She tentatively concludes that the God Warriors were actually created to be gods. (Relevant, because Anno is a Nausicaa fan and did the key frame animation for the melting God Warrior in the movie version.)

[]:             Quote Post by Electreel » Thu Jan 03, 2013 10:14 pm Fair enough, I wasn´t really thinking of Nausicaa at that moment ;) Perhaps the fact that mundane creatures such as the lilin are ruling the planet on their own, without the presence of an active god-like being could be related to the well-known Zarathustra´s concept of " God is dead ", popularized by Nietzsche´s work.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jan 03, 2013 10:32 pm Most of what they do in terms of plot-magic is related to spawning lifeforms - Impact phenomenons are "reset buttons" for example. I don't think they were ever supposed to do much more than spawn or unspawn life, I mean, in all that time before Lillith was found, without a spear to prevent her from anything, she doesn't seem to have done more than stay in the black moon and ooze LCL. There weren't even sufficient "defense mechanisms" to keep those pesky humans from cheerfully extracting the soul.
